name: Raksha Mask (Gara Raksha)
shortDescription: Traditional southern province mask used for protection and health
culturalBackground: The Gara Raksha is one of the most benevolent demons in Sri Lankan folklore, specifically associated with protection and the expulsion of ill luck.
spiritualPurpose: Ward off evil eye and bad luck
modernRelevance: Iconic symbol of the Ambalangoda mask carving tradition

name: Naga Raksha (Cobra Mask)
shortDescription: Fierce mask featuring cobra hoods, symbol of power and protection
culturalBackground: Naga Raksha masks are characterized by the cobra hoods surrounding the face, representing the Naga (serpent) spirit.
spiritualPurpose: Protection from enemy spirits and poison
modernRelevance: Frequently used in cultural displays and museum collections

name: Ginijal Raksha (Fire Mask)
shortDescription: Vibrant mask representing the power of fire and energy
culturalBackground: The Ginijal Raksha represents the destructive yet purifying energy of fire, used to ignite spiritual strength.
modernRelevance: Symbol of intensity and spiritual fervor in local art
